graded_decisions.news_since: Q1 FY2027 earnings on 2026-05-20 beat estimates (EPS $1.87 vs $1.78 est.; revenue $81.6B vs $79.2B; data-centre +100% YoY; $80B buyback authorised), but stock fell for fourth-straight post-earnings session on 'priced for perfection' narrative (CNBC, 2026-05-20)
graded_decisions.comment: Pre-earnings entry; blowout results still produced a sell-the-news reaction. No earnings-guard rule in place; this was a blind bet that a positive surprise would lift the stock.

graded_decisions.news_since: Broad US equities continued multi-week rally; SPY +1.76% close-to-close May 19 to May 22 on easing macro concerns
graded_decisions.comment: Clean entry into broad-market exposure; the only position in the green this week.

graded_decisions.news_since: MSFT reversed sharply May 19 on mixed Xbox messaging and a Windows driver battery-life flaw; high-capex concerns and OpenAI investment losses are near-term overhangs (Yahoo Finance/CNBC, 2026-05-19)
graded_decisions.comment: Bought into a strong open (429.88) that immediately reversed on company-specific negative news. Stock was already +3% over the prior 4 sessions before reversing on the entry day; late entry with no downside protection.

graded_decisions.news_since: Iran War keeps WTI near $90 and Brent near $126; global airlines cut 13,000 flights due to surging jet-fuel costs; XLE up 21.6% YTD but retreating from May 19 intraday peak of $61.49 (BigGo Finance, 2026-05-18)
graded_decisions.comment: Macro thesis (geopolitical oil premium) is intact and well-supported, but entry came 9 days into an unbroken +7% run. Bought a pullback day that continued lower; no stabilisation signal required before entry.

graded_decisions.news_since: Post-earnings drift continues; NVDA -2% since May 20 earnings with no new catalyst to reverse the slide (CNBC, 2026-05-20)
graded_decisions.comment: Second NVDA buy in 4 days while the first lot is still underwater. Added to a losing position without an invalidation framework or new bullish catalyst; the post-earnings slide may not be over.

patterns: Pre-earnings entry, no guard rule: NVDA was bought the day before Q1 FY2027 results. Despite a blowout beat the stock fell for the fourth-straight post-earnings session — 'priced for perfection' consistently defeats positive surprises for mega-cap AI names at current valuations.
Late entries into extended moves: XLE (+7% in 9 sessions before entry) and MSFT (+3% in 4 sessions) were both bought near local highs as those moves were reversing; both immediately went underwater.

Double-loading into a loser: NVDA was purchased on May 19 and again on May 22 while the first lot was still negative. No new catalyst, no confirmation of stabilisation; doubles the position without a thesis reset.
Broad index (SPY) outperformed every individual pick: the only winner was the passive ETF; NVDA, MSFT, and XLE all underperformed, suggesting stock selection added idiosyncratic risk without alpha.
No thesis documentation (first-run journal empty): no entry criteria, price targets, or invalidation levels were recorded for any trade; exits are discretionary rather than rules-based.
Tech concentration risk: NVDA + MSFT represent ~63% of portfolio at cost, creating correlated drawdown exposure if tech sentiment rotates.
suggested_adjustments: Earnings guard: skip new longs (and adding to existing longs) within 2 trading days of a scheduled earnings release; re-enter only after the post-earnings daily close confirms continuation (close > prior close AND > entry price).

Momentum filter before entry: if a symbol has returned >5% in the prior 5 sessions, require either RSI(14) < 60 OR two consecutive consolidation closes (within 1% of each other) before buying. This would have blocked both the XLE and MSFT entries.
Single-sector cap: cap combined notional in any one GICS sector at 40% of portfolio equity; enforce this check before each order and skip the buy if it would be breached. Current IT exposure is ~63%.

Mandatory thesis logging: before each buy, append a decisions.jsonl entry with fields thesis_driver, holding_days_target, and invalidation_trigger. Positions without a logged thesis should be sized at half-normal or skipped.
summary: First review run (journal was empty on first run). Week ending 2026-05-22: portfolio -0.14% vs SPY +1.76%, VT +1.98%, SSO +3.44% — every passive benchmark beat the active book. SPY was the sole winner (+1.81%); NVDA both lots, MSFT, and XLE all lost ground. Key failure modes: pre-earnings blind entry into NVDA, late entries into MSFT and XLE after extended runs, and no stop or invalidation discipline. Four rule adjustments proposed: earnings guard, momentum filter, sector cap, and mandatory thesis logging.
